text:
Certainly. Here's a short story I love:

It was a chilly autumn day. Mrs. Harriet Barnacle wasn't thrilled about attending her neighbor Mr. Oaktree's fancy little party. She glanced out at the falling leaves — orange, amber hues — thinking about cozy evenings indoors. That's when she decided to invite a few furry companions.

Off she went to Mr. Oaktree's garden center. There sat Mrs. Wispsoot, a tiny owl. And then there was Mr. Flutterby, a cheerful hummingbird. And last but not least, a big comfy blanket just waiting for an owl.

By tea time, the birds were fluttering about happily. Mrs. Harriet chuckled as she watched Mr. Flutterby sip tea. She knew each of these little lives was valuable yet silly. Mr. Owl perched meticulously beside her plate. Mr. Wispsook just stared off into space.

They all laughed together. An acorn fell right onto Mr. Flutterby's
